rationale: The cited paper’s focus on latent-class, attribute-level noncompensatory decision rules is plausibly related to the citing paper’s latent-segment, attribute-level SKU choice modeling, so this is not clearly an incidental mismatch. However, the citing note’s stated prior-work anchors emphasize other models (Fader & Hardie; Andrews & Manrai; Erdem), and the matched evidence is only a single DOI reference with no in-text context, so substantive use cannot be confirmed from current evidence.
